methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 0 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 11 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

data.narrative:
Noble Machines is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Wei Ding and Wen-Loong Ma. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of nine private companies and one public comparable (ticker 277810.KQ) operating in the Robotics & Manufacturing sector. Top private peers include 1X Technologies, Divergent, Exotec, Locus Robotics, and Bedrock Robotics.

The peer-median method proved inapplicable to this valuation. Although eleven same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, the narrow filter for seed-stage or adjacent-stage companies with similar valuation tiers yielded fewer than three datapoints, the minimum threshold for robust triangulation. Applying an all-sector median would introduce unacceptable bias given the stage and sector-specific dynamics of frontier robotics. Alternative methods—such as venture capital method benchmarking, revenue multiples, or stage-appropriate precedent transaction analysis—would require additional data on Noble Machines' financial metrics, capital requirements, or recent funding round terms that are not provided here.

Without sufficient narrow-set peer data or target-company financial information, a specific valuation range cannot be supported. The primary uncertainty stems from the absence of comparable seed-stage transactions in robotics. A credible valuation will require either: expansion of the dataset to include more early-stage robotics comps, disclosure of Noble Machines' revenue or ARR, or application of venture capital method assumptions grounded in the target's stated use of proceeds and milestones.
